content.problemStatement:
Chef has 444 positive integers AAA, BBB, CCC, and DDD such that AAA %\%% B=CB=CB=C %\%% DDD.

Find the **smallest positive** integer XXX such that (A+X)(A+X)(A+X) %\%% B=(C+X)B=(C+X)B=(C+X) %\%% DDD holds.

It is guaranteed that such XXX always exists.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers AAA, BBB, CCC, and DDD.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — XXX, the smallest integer satisfying the conditions.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The smallest XXX satisfying the equation is 111, as (4+1)(4+1)(4+1) %\%% 7=(4+1)7=(4+1)7=(4+1) %\%% 8=58 = 58=5.
Test case 222: The smallest XXX satisfying the equation is 222, as (5+2)(5+2)(5+2) %\%% 1=(8+2)1=(8+2)1=(8+2) %\%% 2=02 = 02=0.
